title: 2026 Oscar Winners: Best Costume Design
text: At the 98th Academy Awards ceremony held on March 15, 2026, the Oscar for Best Costume Design went to Kate Hawley for her work on the Netflix film Frankenstein. The other nominees in this category were Deborah L. Scott for Avatar: Fire and Ash, Malgosia Turzanska for Hamnet, Miyako Bellizzi for Marty Supreme, and Ruth E. Carter for Sinners. Hawley's win for Frankenstein was part of the film's successful night at the Oscars, where it secured three total wins including costume design, makeup and hairstyling, and production design. New Zealand costume designer Kate Hawley won the Best Costume Design award at the Academy Awards for the film Frankenstein. Hawley received the award from former Vogue editor Anna Wintour and actor Anne Hathaway. Her elaborate, colour-driven costumes blend gothic aesthetics with Victorian influences, using symbolism and layered textures to enhance the film's narrative and character development inspired by Mary Shelley's classic novel.
